content.text:
<user_query>
Help me to review the sandbox configuration of gh-aw: https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/ Will it change the `MCP_GATEWAY_PORT="80"

In practice, I have multiple agentic workflows running at the same self-hosted runners, it will fail because of the port 80 occupied. I'm wondering if any way to resolve it.

Help me to dig into it. make a clear map that how multiple agentic workflows running at the same runner at the same time.
</user_query>

Researching GitHub's gh-aw sandbox docs and scanning the repo for `MCP_GATEWAY_PORT` and related configuration.

[REDACTED]

Tracing how frontmatter maps to the generated workflow and whether we can override the gateway port.

[REDACTED]

Checking whether the activation job uses a different `runs-on` and how self-hosted jobs map:

[REDACTED]

Creating a plan that maps official sandbox docs to the compiled workflow, explains port 80 conflicts, and lists mitigations.

[REDACTED]

input.name: gh-aw MCP port concurrency
input.overview: The official sandbox reference controls the MCP gateway listen port via frontmatter (`sandbox.mcp.port`), which the compiler turns into the `MCP_GATEWAY_PORT` used at runtime. Port 80 conflicts on one host come from `--network host` plus a fixed port and shared `/tmp/gh-aw`, not from editing the lockfile by hand.
input.plan:
# gh-aw sandbox, MCP gateway port, and concurrent self-hosted jobs

## Direct answer: does the sandbox reference change `MCP_GATEWAY_PORT="80"`?

**Indirectly, yes — via frontmatter, not by setting an env var yourself.**

Per [Sandbox Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/), the supported knob is **`sandbox.mcp.port`** (with **`features.mcp-gateway: true`** when you opt into the gateway block in the doc). The compiler emits a step that **`export MCP_GATEWAY_PORT="<that value>"`** and feeds the same value into the gateway JSON (`"gateway": { "port": ... }`).

Your repo’s compiled workflow hardcodes **80** because the source frontmatter (e.g. [`.github/workflows/q.md`](.github/workflows/q.md)) does **not** set `sandbox.mcp` — so the compiler’s default is **80**, matching your own doc line that the agent reaches the gateway at `http://host.docker.internal:80` ([`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md)).

**Do not hand-edit** [`q.lock.yml`](.github/workflows/q.lock.yml) (it says “DO NOT EDIT”). Change the **AW markdown frontmatter**, then re-run **`gh aw compile`** (or your usual update path) so the lockfile is regenerated.

Example aligned with GitHub’s doc:

```yaml
features:
  mcp-gateway: true
sandbox:
  agent: awf   # default; keep explicit if you want
  mcp:
    port: 8080
    api-key: "${{ secrets.MCP_GATEWAY_API_KEY }}"
```

After changing the port, anything that assumes **`:80`** in prose or host setup must be updated to the new port (Copilot/driver reads the generated `mcp-config.json`, but operators often still think in terms of “port 80”).

---

## Why multiple agentic jobs on the same host fight over port 80

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph host [Self_hosted_physical_or_VM]
    subgraph jobA [Job_A_runner_workdir]
      RTA["RUNNER_TEMP/gh-aw unique per job"]
      TMP["/tmp/gh-aw SHARED on host"]
      GA["MCP gateway container gh-aw-mcpg"]
    end
    subgraph jobB [Job_B_runner_workdir]
      RTB["RUNNER_TEMP/gh-aw unique per job"]
      TMP2["/tmp/gh-aw SAME path as Job A"]
      GB["Second MCP gateway"]
    end
    NS["Host network namespace"]
  end
  GA -->|"docker --network host bind :PORT"| NS
  GB -->|"docker --network host bind :PORT"| NS
  jobA --> TMP
  jobB --> TMP2
```

1. **MCP gateway uses host networking** — In your lockfile, `MCP_GATEWAY_DOCKER_COMMAND` includes **`--network host`** (see [`.github/workflows/q.lock.yml`](.github/workflows/q.lock.yml) around the “Start MCP Gateway” step). That means the gateway listens on a **real host TCP port**, not an isolated bridge port per job.

2. **Default port is 80** — `export MCP_GATEWAY_PORT="80"` in the same step. Two concurrent jobs both try to bind **host:80** → second start fails (“address already in use”) unless something else fails first.

3. **`/tmp/gh-aw` is shared** — Both jobs use fixed paths like `/tmp/gh-aw/mcp-config`, prompts, logs. Even if ports differed, concurrent runs on one machine risk **filesystem cross-talk** unless GitHub/gh-aw isolates per run (today’s generated YAML is oriented around a single tree under `/tmp/gh-aw`). Your [agentic workflows guide](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) already calls out **`RUNNER_TEMP` must not be `/tmp`**; parallel jobs are a separate dimension from `RUNNER_TEMP`.

4. **How jobs land on the host** — GitHub assigns each queued job to **a runner**. Multiple **runner processes** on the same machine (e.g. `count: 2` in [host-setup](docs/content/host-setup.md)) can run **two jobs at once**, so the collisions above are realistic. A **single** runner process still runs one job at a time, but you can still get overlap if multiple hosts are misread as “one runner” or if jobs retry/overlap during handoff.

5. **Workflow-level concurrency** — Your `q` lockfile uses `concurrency.group: gh-aw-${{ github.workflow }}-...` which **serializes invocations of that workflow** per issue/PR, but **does not** stop **`plan`** vs **`q`** vs other AW workflows from running **at the same time** on the same self-hosted host.

---

## Practical ways to resolve port 80 occupation

| Approach | Effect |
|----------|--------|
| **Set distinct `sandbox.mcp.port` values per AW workflow** (compile each `.md`) | Avoids 80 and can spread workflows across e.g. 8080, 8081, … **Still fails** if two concurrent runs use the **same** workflow + same port. |
| **Cap concurrent agentic jobs per host to 1** | Simplest operational fix: one runner registration per machine for agentic labels, or tune org/repo concurrency so only one gh-aw agent job runs per host. |
| **Separate isolation units** | Ephemeral runner per job, or one VM/container per runner — each host has at most one gateway binding that port. |
| **Coordinate with GitHub / gh-aw** | If you need **N concurrent jobs on one host** with **dynamic** gateway ports, that would require product/compiler support (today’s emitted YAML uses a **compile-time** port, not a random per-run port like Safe Outputs’ HTTP server). |

---

## Files to treat as source of truth in this repo

- **Operator intent / docs**: [Sandbox reference](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/) for `sandbox.mcp.port` + `features.mcp-gateway`.
- **What actually runs**: generated [`*.lock.yml`](.github/workflows/q.lock.yml) under `.github/workflows/` — search `MCP_GATEWAY_PORT` and `Start MCP Gateway`.
- **Frontmatter you control**: each [`*.md`](.github/workflows/q.md) under `.github/workflows/` (remember `source:` may point at upstream; compile behavior follows your `gh aw` version, currently **v0.68.3** per lockfile header).

No code changes are required for this review unless you choose to add `sandbox.mcp` to your workflow sources and recompile.

todos.content: Add features.mcp-gateway + sandbox.mcp.port to each AW .md that must coexist on one host; pick unique ports per workflow if parallel runs are possible
Run gh aw compile (or project compile script) and confirm lock steps show new MCP_GATEWAY_PORT and gateway JSON port
Align runner count / labels / repo concurrency so only one agentic job binds host-network MCP per machine, or use isolated VMs per concurrent job
If port != 80, update docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md references to host.docker.internal:80 and any runbooks

content.text:
<user_query>
If I set `sandbox.agent: fase`, will it turn the AWF off?
</user_query>

Yes — with the spelling **`sandbox.agent: false`** (not `fase`).

From GitHub’s [Sandbox Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/) docs:

- **`sandbox.agent: awf`** — Uses **AWF** as the coding-agent sandbox (default if you omit `sandbox`).
- **`sandbox.agent: false`** — **Turns off the coding-agent sandbox**; the doc describes this as disabling the **agent firewall only**, with **less isolation**, and explicitly says the **MCP gateway stays on** and *cannot* be disabled from there.

So **`false` means AWF is not used for the agent** (no AWF-style container/firewall path for that part). It does **not** mean “disable all sandboxing everywhere” — MCP gateway routing remains active per that reference.

Use **`false` only when you really need it**; the docs mark it as requiring caution because security isolation is reduced.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
We cannot fix gh-aw, but could we make `gh-sr` help to organize the workflow sandbox.mcp.port? We could do it based on the workflows runs-on self-host runner and the `gh sr config`, so that ensure that all workflows could run perfactly.
</user_query>

Exploring how `gh-sr` models runners and config so we can plan integration for assigning distinct `sandbox.mcp.port` values.

[REDACTED]

Checking how `doctor` loads repo context and whether a repo path exists in config:

[REDACTED]

Noting a constraint: identical workflows on the same host share one compiled `sandbox.mcp.port`, so per-runner labels alone can't fix concurrent duplicate workflows without upstream changes or duplicate workflow definitions.

[REDACTED]

input.name: gh-sr MCP port orchestration
input.overview: Extend gh-sr to (1) optionally assign stable MCP gateway ports and matching runner labels per agentic instance on a host, and (2) lint or patch AW workflow frontmatter so `sandbox.mcp.port` and `runs-on` stay consistent—while documenting that same-workflow concurrent runs on one host remain fundamentally limited without gh-aw changes.
input.plan:
# gh-sr: orchestrate `sandbox.mcp.port` with self-hosted config

## Problem restatement

- MCP gateway uses **host networking**; the listen port comes from **`sandbox.mcp.port`** in AW frontmatter, compiled into steps like `MCP_GATEWAY_PORT` (see prior analysis on [`.github/workflows/q.lock.yml`](.github/workflows/q.lock.yml)).
- Today [`internal/config/config.go`](internal/config/config.go) gives **every instance** of a runner (`name-1`, `name-2`, … from [`InstanceNames`](internal/config/config.go)) the **same** [`EffectiveLabels`](internal/config/config.go). Two agentic jobs on one host can still bind the **same** port if they use the **same** workflow artifact.
- gh-sr cannot fix gh-aw itself; it **can** coordinate **config + labels + repo frontmatter** so that **distinct** workflows (or **deliberately duplicated** workflow files) use **distinct** ports and route to runners that advertise matching labels.

## What “perfectly” can and cannot mean

| Scenario | Solvable by gh-sr + frontmatter? |
|----------|----------------------------------|
| Multiple **different** AW `.md` workflows may run **concurrently** on the **same host** | **Yes**, if each has a **unique** `sandbox.mcp.port` and jobs are routed so only compatible combinations run (labels). |
| Multiple **concurrent jobs of the same compiled workflow** (same port) on the **same host** | **No** (not without **gh-aw** dynamic ports or **N copies** of the workflow with different ports / matrix). Plan should **warn** in `doctor` when `count > 1` for agentic on one host and repo uses a **single** default port. |

## Proposed design (two layers)

### Layer A — Runner config + registration (gh-sr YAML)

Add optional fields on [`RunnerConfig`](internal/config/config.go) (names bikesheddable), for example:

- `agentic_mcp_ports: [9080, 9081]` — length **must** equal `count` when set (validate in [`Validate`](internal/config/config.go)).
- Or `agentic_mcp_port_base: 9080` — derive `9080 + (instanceIndex-1)` when `count > 1`.

**Behavior when `profile: agentic` and ports are configured:**

1. Extend label computation (today in [`EffectiveLabels`](internal/config/config.go) + [`applyAgenticDefaults`](internal/config/config.go)) so **each instance** gets a unique label, e.g. `gh-sr-mcp-9081`, derived from that instance’s port (in addition to existing `agentic`).
2. Thread **instance index** into native config scripts in [`internal/runner/native.go`](internal/runner/native.go) (`config.sh` / `config.cmd` `--labels`) so `aw-runner-1` and `aw-runner-2` register with **different** labels today they share one label string from `rc.EffectiveLabels(...)`.

This ties **machine-side** capacity (N listeners) to **GitHub routing** (N label sets).

### Layer B — Repo workflow frontmatter (source `.md`)

Implement a small parser (new package, e.g. `internal/ghawfrontmatter`) that:

- Finds `.github/workflows/*.md` under a user-supplied **`--workflow-root`** (default `.`), splits YAML frontmatter between `---` fences.
- Reads `sandbox.mcp.port`, `features.mcp-gateway`, `runs-on`, `runs-on-slim` (per [docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) §8).

**Subcommands** (under e.g. `gh sr aw` in [`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go)):

1. **`gh sr aw ports check`** — Read-only: report duplicate/missing ports, list workflows defaulting to implicit 80, and **cross-check** against loaded config:
   - Group [`config.RunnerConfig`](internal/config/config.go) by `Host` for `profile: agentic`.
   - For each host, `maxConcurrent = sum(count)` across matching runners (or filtered by `--repo`).
   - WARN if `maxConcurrent > 1` and fewer distinct `sandbox.mcp.port` values exist than needed for user’s declared concurrency model; WARN if `runs-on` cannot satisfy label-per-port runners (missing `gh-sr-mcp-*` in `runs-on` when config emits those labels).

2. **`gh sr aw ports assign --write`** (optional, explicit flag) — Deterministic port assignment for **each** `*.md` (e.g. stable sort by path, assign 9080, 9081, …), set `features.mcp-gateway: true` and `sandbox.mcp.port` if absent. **Does not** auto-fix `runs-on`; print follow-up instructions or a second mode `--suggest-runs-on` that only **prints** YAML snippets (safer than rewriting `runs-on` blindly).

Users still run **`gh aw compile`** locally/CI after edits (gh-sr does not replace the compiler).

### Layer C — Doctor integration

In [`internal/doctor/doctor.go`](internal/doctor/doctor.go), add an optional section **“Agentic MCP ports”** when `--workflow-root` is passed (or always try `./.github/workflows` if it exists **and** `--repo` matches a runner repo): delegate to the same `ports check` logic so `gh sr doctor` surfaces conflicts without a new command.

## Data flow (high level)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph cfg [runners.yml]
    RC[RunnerConfig profile agentic]
    MP[agentic_mcp_ports or base]
  end
  subgraph reg [GitHub registration]
    L1[labels incl gh-sr-mcp-9080]
    L2[labels incl gh-sr-mcp-9081]
  end
  subgraph repo [.github/workflows/*.md]
    FM[sandbox.mcp.port + runs-on]
  end
  RC --> MP
  MP --> L1
  MP --> L2
  FM -->|"gh sr aw ports check"| X[lint output]
  L1 --> X
  L2 --> X
```

## Testing strategy

- Unit tests for: YAML frontmatter extraction edge cases; port list validation (`len(ports)==count`); label generation per instance; `ports check` duplicate detection.
- Golden-file or snapshot tests optional for CLI output fragments.

## Documentation

- Extend [docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md): new subsection on **concurrent agentic jobs**, `gh-sr-mcp-*` labels, `gh sr aw ports`, and the **same-workflow same-host** limitation.

## Open product choices (implementer picks defaults)

- Label prefix (`gh-sr-mcp-` vs shorter).
- Whether `ports assign` is behind `--write` only (recommended) defaulting to dry-run.
- Whether port config lives on `RunnerConfig` or a new top-level `agentic:` block (block is nicer if you later add more gh-aw-related knobs).

todos.content: Add RunnerConfig fields for per-instance MCP ports; validate len vs count in config.Validate
Plumb instance index into native config.sh/cmd label generation; append gh-sr-mcp-<port> for agentic
New internal package: parse .github/workflows/*.md frontmatter for sandbox.mcp.port, runs-on, features
Add cobra command gh sr aw ports check|assign; optional doctor --workflow-root integration
Update agentic-workflows.md; add unit tests for parser, validation, label diffs

```
## 9. What `profile: agentic` automates
input.new_string:
threat-detection:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest  # optionally run threat detection on GitHub-hosted
---
```

### 8a. Concurrent jobs, MCP gateway ports, and gh-sr

The gh-aw MCP gateway listens on the host network (`docker --network host`). The TCP port comes from workflow frontmatter `sandbox.mcp.port` (default **80** after `gh aw compile`). If several agentic jobs run on the **same physical host**, they must use **different** ports, or only one such job may run at a time.

**Configure per-instance ports in gh-sr** (`runners.yml`): on a runner with `profile: agentic`, set either:

- `agentic_mcp_port_base: 9080` — instances get ports `9080`, `9081`, … up to `count-1`, or
- `agentic_mcp_ports: [9080, 9081]` — explicit list whose length must equal `count`.

gh-sr then registers each instance with an extra label **`gh-sr-mcp-<port>`** (for example `gh-sr-mcp-9080`) so GitHub can route jobs to a runner whose MCP port matches the workflow.

Match the workflow frontmatter to that port and label:

```yaml
features:
  mcp-gateway: true
sandbox:
  mcp:
    port: 9080
runs-on:
  - self-hosted
  - Linux
  - X64
  - agentic
  - gh-sr-mcp-9080
```

**CLI helpers** (run from a clone of the repository that contains `.github/workflows/*.md`):

- `gh sr aw ports check [--workflow-root DIR] [--repo owner/repo]` — warns on duplicate ports, implicit default 80 across files, and rough concurrency vs. `count` on each host.
- `gh sr aw ports assign [--workflow-root DIR] [--base-port 9080] [--write]` — assigns distinct `sandbox.mcp.port` values (dry-run unless `--write`). Re-run `gh aw compile` afterward.
- `gh sr doctor --workflow-root DIR` (or `doctor --repo owner/repo` when `./.github/workflows` exists in the current directory) — runs the same MCP port checks after host diagnostics.

**Limitation:** two concurrent jobs that use the **same compiled workflow** (same `sandbox.mcp.port`) on one host still conflict; fixing that requires different workflow sources, separate machines, limiting concurrency, or changes in gh-aw.

## 9. What `profile: agentic` automates

input.old_string:
| **`agentic` label** | Appends `agentic` to runner labels | Routes agentic workflow jobs to this runner |

## 10. End-to-End Verification Script
input.new_string:
| **`agentic` label** | Appends `agentic` to runner labels | Routes agentic workflow jobs to this runner |
| **`gh-sr-mcp-*` labels** | When `agentic_mcp_port_base` or `agentic_mcp_ports` is set, each instance gets `gh-sr-mcp-<port>` | Lets `runs-on` target a runner whose MCP listener matches `sandbox.mcp.port` (see [§8a](#8a-concurrent-jobs-mcp-gateway-ports-and-gh-sr)) |

## 10. End-to-End Verification Script

input.description: Re-test ghawports and gofmt touched Go files

todos.content: New internal package: parse .github/workflows/*.md frontmatter for sandbox.mcp.port, runs-on, features
Add cobra command gh sr aw ports check|assign; optional doctor --workflow-root integration
Update agentic-workflows.md; add unit tests for parser, validation, label diffs

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented:

### Config ([`internal/config/config.go`](internal/config/config.go))
- New optional fields on `RunnerConfig`: **`agentic_mcp_ports`** (length must equal `count`) and **`agentic_mcp_port_base`** (ports `base`, `base+1`, …). Mutually exclusive; only valid with **`profile: agentic`**.
- Constant **`AgenticMCPLabelPrefix`** = `gh-sr-mcp-`.
- **`AgenticMCPPortsResolved`**, **`InstanceCount`**, **`EffectiveLabelsForInstance(hostOS, arch, instanceIndex)`** — adds `gh-sr-mcp-<port>` per instance when ports are set.
- **`EffectiveLabels`** now delegates to **`EffectiveLabelsForInstance(..., 0)`** (same as before when no MCP port strategy).

### Runner registration ([`internal/runner/native.go`](internal/runner/native.go), [`internal/runner/runner.go`](internal/runner/runner.go))
- **`setupNative`** / **`windowsNativeConfigScript`** use **`EffectiveLabelsForInstance(..., i)`** so each instance gets the correct **`gh-sr-mcp-*`** label at **`config.sh` / `config.cmd`** time.
- **`Status`** shows per-instance labels.

### Frontmatter + checks ([`internal/ghawfrontmatter/`](internal/ghawfrontmatter/ghawfrontmatter.go), [`internal/ghawports/`](internal/ghawports/check.go))
- Parses AW-style `*.md` under **`.github/workflows`**, reads **`sandbox.mcp.port`**, **`features.mcp-gateway`**, **`runs-on`**, **`EffectiveMCPPort()`** (default **80**).
- **`ghawports.Check`**: duplicate MCP ports across workflows; host **`count`** vs distinct self-hosted ports; with **`--repo`**, warns if a workflow’s port is in the runner’s resolved port list but **`runs-on`** lacks **`gh-sr-mcp-<port>`**.
- **`ghawports.Assign`**: deterministic **`sandbox.mcp.port`** + **`features.mcp-gateway: true`** (dry-run unless **`--write`**).

### CLI ([`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go))
- **`gh sr aw ports check`** — respects global **`--config`**, **`--repo`**, **`--workflow-root`**.
- **`gh sr aw ports assign`** — **`--base-port`** (default **9080**), **`--write`**.
- **`gh sr doctor --workflow-root`**; if **`--workflow-root`** is omitted but **`--repo`** is set and **`./.github/workflows`** exists, doctor uses **``.`** for that lint ([`internal/doctor/doctor.go`](internal/doctor/doctor.go)).

### Docs ([`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md))
- New **§8a** on concurrent runs, **`agentic_mcp_port_base` / `agentic_mcp_ports`**, labels, CLI, and the same-workflow / same-host limitation; table in §9 extended.

### Tests
- [`internal/ghawfrontmatter/ghawfrontmatter_test.go`](internal/ghawfrontmatter/ghawfrontmatter_test.go), [`internal/ghawports/check_test.go`](internal/ghawports/check_test.go), new cases in [`internal/config/config_test.go`](internal/config/config_test.go). **`doctor.Run`** / dashboard call sites updated for the new parameter.

After **`gh sr aw ports assign --write`**, run **`gh aw compile`** as usual so lockfiles pick up new ports. Re-register or **`gh sr setup`** runners when you add **`agentic_mcp_*`** so GitHub gets the new **`gh-sr-mcp-*`** labels.

[REDACTED]
